label: molecular skeleton joining reaction
definition: A reaction where the number of atoms in the molecular skeleton increases.

label: planned reaction step
definition: A molecular process with which the experimenters aim to achieve a particular molecular transformation.

label: carbon-carbon coupling reaction
definition: A reaction where a bond is formed between two carbon atoms.

label: Perkin reaction
definition: An aldol condensation where an aryl aldehyde reacts with an acid anhydride to form a cinnamic acid.

label: obsolete: ring reaction
definition: A reaction in which the number of atoms in any ring changes between the reactant and the products.

label: ring formation reaction step
definition: A planned reaction step where the product contains a ring that was not in any of the reactants.

label: Diels-Alder reaction
definition: A [4+2] cycloaddition reaction where a diene reacts with a compound containing a double or triple bond (a dienophile) to form an unsaturated six-membered ring compound.

label: ene reaction
definition: A joining reaction where an alkene with an allylic hydrogen reacts with a compound containing a multiple bond (an enophile) to form a substituted alkene.

label: conjugate addition
definition: A joining reaction where a nucleophile adds to the beta-carbon of an alpha,beta-unsaturated system (or an activated carbon-carbon bond).

label: Michael addition
definition: A conjugate addition reaction where a carbon nucleophile adds to the beta-carbon of an alpha,beta-unsaturated system (or an activated carbon-carbon bond).

label: functional modification
definition: A reaction where the number of atoms in the skeleton, and their connectivity, remain the same.

label: functional group modification
definition: A reaction in which a functional group undergoes a change that does not affect the number of atoms in (or connectivity of) the skeleton of the molecule.

label: functional group oxidation
definition: A functional group modification reaction where a functional group is oxidised.

label: diazotisation
definition: A functional group oxidation where an aromatic amine is transformed into an aromatic diazonium salt.

label: Grignard reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where a Grignard reagent reacts with a compound containing an electrophilic carbon atom. A Grignard reagent is a magnesium-stabilised carbon nucleophile. An aldehyde leads to a secondary alcohol, a ketone leads to a tertiary alcohol, an ester leads to a ketone and a nitrile leads to a ketone.

label: Wittig reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where a carbonyl compound reacts with a phosphonium ylide to give an alkene.

label: aldol addition
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an enol or enolate reacts with a carbonyl compound to form a beta-hydroxy ketone. The name comes from aldehyde + alcohol.

label: aldol condensation
definition: An aldol addition where an enol or enolate reacts with a carbonyl compound to form an alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone.

label: 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition
definition: A ring formation reaction where a 1,3-dipolar compound reacts with an alkene or alkyne to form a five-membered ring compound.

label: Sandmeyer reaction
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(I) halide or pseudohalide to form an aromatic halide or pseudohalide.

label: Gattermann halogenation
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with copper powder and hydrochloric acid or hydrobromic acid to form an aromatic chloride or bromide.

label: Koerner-Contardi reaction
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(II) halide to form an aromatic halide.

label: Heck reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an organohalide or triflate couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin, catalysed by palladium. It is stereospecific.

label: rearrangement step
definition: A reaction which preserves the number of rings and number of skeletal atoms and in which the molecule rearranges so that one or more atoms or groups of atoms move from one atom to another.

label: Beckmann rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement where an oxime rearranges to form an amide. Often there is selective migration of one group.

label: Cope rearrangement
definition: A [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement where a 1,5-hexadiene rearranges to form a 1,5-hexadiene.

label: oxy-Cope rearrangement
definition: A [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of a 3-hydroxy-1,5-hexadiene rearranges to form a 5,6-unsaturated carbonyl compound.

label: molecular skeleton insertion reaction
definition: A joining reaction in which one or more atoms are added to the middle of a chain.

label: Baeyer-Villiger oxidation
definition: A reaction of a ketone to give an ester, in which an alkyl, benzyl or phenyl group migrates from one of the alpha-carbon centres to an adjacent oxygen, displacing an oxygen-based leaving group; the result is a formal insertion of an oxygen atom. The reaction also works with aldehydes, giving the corresponding carboxylic acid. The term "Baeyer-Villiger rearrangement" may occasionally be used in a mechanistic sense to refer specificially to the actual rearrangement step.

label: Mannich reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where ammonia or an amine reacts with a non-enolisable aldehyde, often methanal, and an enolisable carbonyl compound to form a beta-amino carbonyl compound.

label: Mitsunobu reaction
definition: A substitution where an alcohol is converted into something else. Typical reagents are a dialkyl azodicarboxylate and a triarylphosphine. The alcohol stereocentre is inverted.

label: Stille coupling
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where a stannane reacts with an organohalide or organotriflate.

label: Reformatsky reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an aldehyde or amine reacts with a alpha-halo ester and zinc to form a beta-hydroxy ester.

label: functional group reduction
definition: A functional group modification reaction where a functional group is reduced.

label: Clemmensen reduction
definition: A functional group reduction where an aldehyde or ketone carbonyl group is reduced to a methylene with zinc-mercury amalgam and hydrochloric acid.

label: carbon-carbon homocoupling reaction
definition: A joining reaction where a carbon-carbon bond is formed between like moieties.

label: Ullmann reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon homocoupling reaction of an aryl halide mediated by copper.

label: addition reaction step
definition: A reaction in which two or more groups of atoms are added to the molecule, resulting in a change of skeletal bond order.

label: Birch reduction
definition: Addition of hydrogen atoms to aromatic rings by means of alkali metals in liquid ammonia.

label: Claisen condensation
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction of an ester and a carbonyl compound to form a beta-keto ester.

label: Knoevenagel condensation
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction of an aldehyde and a methylene compound.

label: Friedel-Crafts acylation
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between an arene and a carbonyl compound (usually an acyl halide) to give an aryl ketone. The reaction is catalysed by a Lewis acid.

label: Friedel-Crafts alkylation
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between an arene and an alkyl halide/alkene/alcohol (anything that can form a carbocation) to give a substituted arene. The reaction is catalysed by a Lewis acid.

label: Oppenauer oxidation
definition: An oxidation of a secondary alcohol to a ketone involving an equilibrium between the alcohol and a reagent ketone present in excess, catalysed by a metal alkoxide.

label: Prins reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling between formaldehyde and an alkene to give a 1,3-diol or an allylic alcohol, catalysed by acid.

label: intramolecular Prins reaction
definition: A reaction between formaldehyde and an alkene to give a 1,3-dioxane, catalysed by acid.

label: pinacol rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a 1,2-diol to give a carbonyl compound, catalysed by acid.

label: Wolff rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a diazoketone to give a ketene, with the elimination of molecular nitrogen.

label: fused-ring-system formation
definition: A reaction in which a fused ring system is present in a product that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: Bischler-Napieralski reaction
definition: A cyclodehydration of a beta-phenethylamide to give a 3,4-dihydroisoquinoline.

label: Curtius rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of an acyl azide to give an isocyanate by pyrolysis, with the elimination of molecular nitrogen.

label: Vilsmeier-Haack reaction
definition: A formylation of an activated arene with a disubstituted formamide to give an arenecarbaldehyde.

label: Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a phosphonate and a carbonyl compound to give an olefin.

label: Horner reaction
definition: A joining reaction between a phosphine oxide and a carbonyl compound to give an olefin.

label: Ritter reaction
definition: A reaction between an alcohol or alkene and a nitrile to give an amide, catalysed by a Bronsted acid.

label: Pictet-Spengler reaction
definition: A condensation-cyclisation of a beta-phenethylamine with an aldehyde to give a tetrahydroisoquinoline.

label: Arbuzov reaction
definition: The alkylation of a trialkyl phosphite with an alkyl halide or acyl halide to give an alkyl phosphonate.

label: Mukaiyama aldol condensation
definition: An aldol condensation between an aldehyde or ketone and a silyl enol ether (also known as a ketene silyl acetal) to give a beta-hydroxy carbonyl compound.

label: Skraup reaction
definition: A condensation between an aromatic amine and glycerol to give a quinoline.

label: Arndt-Eistert reaction
definition: A homologation reaction of a carboxylic acid to give the homologated carboxylic acid via acyl chloride and diazoketone intermediates.

label: Fischer indole synthesis
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde or ketone with a phenylhydrazine to give an indole.

label: Dieckmann cyclisation
definition: An intramolecular cyclisation of a diester to give a cyclic beta-ketoester.

label: Staudinger reaction
definition: A joining of an organic azide and a tertiary phosphine to give a phosphazo compound.

label: benzidine rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a hydrazobenzene to give a diaminobiphenyl compound.

label: semidine rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a p-substituted hydrazobenzene to give a p-aminodiphenylamine.

label: Dimroth rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a heterocyclic compound in which endocyclic and exocyclic heteroatoms swap places.

label: chain lengthening
definition: A molecular skeleton joining reaction in which a chain part of a molecular skeleton is lengthened.

label: Edman degradation
definition: A cleaving reaction in which a peptide is treated with phenylisothiocyanate, removing the terminal amino acid group, to form the chain-shortened amine and a phenylthiohydantoin.

label: Reimer-Tiemann reaction
definition: A formylation reaction between a phenol and chloroform (for example, to form an ortho-formyl phenol).

label: cleaving reaction
definition: A reaction where atoms are lost from the molecular skeleton.

label: Wurtz reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon homocoupling reaction of two molecules of an alkyl halide with sodium to give a symmetrical product.

label: benzilic acid rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a 1,2-diketone to an alpha-hydroxy carboxylic acid. It can also be applied to alpha-keto aldehydes. The first exmaple of this reaction was with benzil (PhC(=O)C(=O)Ph) to form benzilic acid, hence the name.

label: Baylis-Hillman reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling of an aldehyde to an activated alkene in the presence of a nucleophilic base to give an alpha-hydroxyalkylated compound.

label: Darzens reaction
definition: A coupling of an aldehyde or ketone with an alpha-halo ester to form an alpha,beta-epoxy ester, which hydrolyses to yield aldehydes or ketones by elimination of carbon dioxide.

label: protection reaction
definition: A reaction in which a functional group is modified by converting it into a protecting group, in order to make subsequent reactions more selective.

label: Boc protection
definition: A protection reaction in which an amine is converted into a tert-butoxycarbonyl compound.

label: Peterson olefination
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an alpha-silyl carbanion reacts with an aldehyde or a ketone to give an alkene, proceeding via a beta-hydroxysilane. The stereochemistry of the alkene can be controlled by treating the intermediate with acid or base.

label: Ullmann condensation
definition: A coupling reaction in which a phenol is coupled to an aryl halide to give a diaryl ether in the presence of a copper compound.

label: Goldberg reaction
definition: A coupling reaction in which an acetanilide is coupled to an aryl halide to give an N-acetyl diaryl amine in the presence of a copper compound.

label: Paternò–Büchi reaction
definition: A photochemical cycloaddition between an aldehyde or ketone and an alkene to give an oxetane.

label: Barbier reaction
definition: A joining reaction between an aldehyde or ketone and an alkyl halide or aryl halide or vinyl halide or allyl halide in the presence of metal to give a secondary or tertiary alcohol.

label: acyloin condensation
definition: A reductive coupling reaction between two esters to give an acyloin (an alpha-hydroxyketone).

label: Henry reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a nitroalkane and an aldehyde or ketone to give a beta-hydroxy nitroalkane.

label: Barton reaction
definition: The photolytic oxidation of a nitrate ester to give a gamma-nitroalcohol.

label: Negishi coupling
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between an organohalide or organotriflate and an organozinc compound. The reaction is catalysed by palladium or nickel.

label: Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction
definition: A reduction of an aldehyde or ketone to an alcohol involving an equilibrium between the aldehyde or ketone and a reagent alcohol present in excess, catalysed by a metal alkoxide. This reaction is the reverse of the Oppenauer oxidation (RXNO:0000047).

label: Williamson ether synthesis
definition: The reaction between an alkyl halide or alkyl sulfate and a metal alkoxide to give an ether.

label: Wittig methylenation
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where a carbonyl compound reacts with a phosphonium ylide to give a terminal alkene.

label: aza-Diels-Alder reaction
definition: A Diels-Alder reaction between a diene and an imine or an azadiene and an alkene to give a six-membered nitrogen-containing ring compound.

label: extrusion reaction
definition: A cleaving reaction in which a skeletal atom or group of atoms is removed from the middle of a molecule.

label: Ramberg-Bäcklund reaction
definition: The reaction of an alpha-halosulfone to give an alkene, with the extrusion of sulfur dioxide.

label: Fischer-Hepp rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of an aromatic nitrosamine to a para-nitrosoaniline.

label: azo coupling
definition: A coupling reaction of a diazonium salt with a phenol or aniline to give an azo compound.

label: imino-Reformatsky reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an imine reacts with a alpha-halo ester and zinc to form a beta-amino ester.

label: Glaser coupling
definition: A coupling reaction between two molecules of a terminal alkyne catalysed by Cu(I) salts.

label: Eglinton reaction
definition: A coupling reaction between two molecules of a terminal alkyne using stoichiometric Cu(II) salts.

label: Cadiot-Chodkiewicz coupling
definition: A coupling reaction between a terminal alkyne and an alkynyl halide catalysed by Cu(I) salts.

label: Leuckart reaction
definition: The reductive amination of ketones or aldehydes with an ammonium formate or formamide to form an amine.

label: Leuckart-Wallach reaction
definition: The reductive amination of ketones or aldehydes with an amine and formic acid to form amines.

label: Gabriel synthesis
definition: The reaction of primary alkyl halides with sodium or potassium phthalimide, followed by hydrolysis, to give the corresponding primary amine.

label: Ing-Manske procedure
definition: The reaction of primary alkyl halides with sodium or potassium phthalimide, followed by treatment with hydrazine, to give the corresponding primary amine.

label: end-of-chain chain shortening
definition: A reaction in which a single skeletal atom is removed from the end of a chain.

label: Hunsdiecker reaction
definition: The decarboxylation of silver carboxylates with halogens to give organic halides.

label: malonic ester synthesis
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a dialkyl malonate and an alkyl halide, to give a substituted acetic acid. The base/R'X treatment can be repeated to give a disubstituted product.

label: ring expansion
definition: A reaction in which an existing ring is increased in size.

label: Pfitzinger reaction
definition: The reaction between isatin and an alpha-methylene carbonyl compound to give a substituted quinoline-4-carboxylic acid.

label: fragmentation reaction
definition: A reaction in which the parent molecule breaks up into three or more products.

label: Grob fragmentation
definition: The fragmentation of a molecule that takes place when an electron-donating fragment (X) and an electron-accepting fragment (X') are situated at positions 1 and 3 on an aliphatic chain, to give a cationic fragment, a neutral fragment (e.g. an alkene) and an anionic fragment.

label: Brook rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of an alpha-, beta- or gamma-hydroxy silane to give a silyl ether. It is catalysed by a base.

label: Nicholas reaction
definition: The reaction of a propargylic cation (stabilised with dicobalt octacarbonyl) with a nucleophile to give a substituted alkyne.

label: Nicholas reaction, nucleophilic heteroatom centre
definition: The reaction of a propargylic cation (stabilised with dicobalt octacarbonyl) with a hydride or amine or enamine to give a substituted alkyne.

label: Nicholas reaction, nucleophilic carbon centre
definition: The reaction of a propargylic cation (stabilised with dicobalt octacarbonyl) with a nucleophilic carbon centre (on e.g. enolates, allyl silanes, ketones, electron-rich aromatics, beta-dicarbonyl compounds) to give a substituted alkyne.

label: Julia olefination
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a phenyl sulfone and an aldehyde or ketone to give a (typically trans) alkene.

label: Sommelet reaction
definition: The oxidation of a benzylic halide to give an aldehyde. The reaction uses hexamethylenetetramine.

label: Bouveault-Blanc reaction
definition: The reduction of an ester using sodium to give two alcohols.

label: Payne rearrangement
definition: A base-catalysed isomerisation of a 2,3-epoxyalcohol, with inversion of configuration at C2.

label: aza-Payne rearrangement
definition: A base-catalysed isomerisation of a 2,3-epoxyamine to form a hydroxy aziridine, with inversion of configuration at C2.

label: Baker-Venkataraman rearrangement
definition: The base-catalysed rearrangement of an o-acetoxyacetophenone to give a hydroxyphenyl-1,3-diketone.

label: molecular skeleton elimination reaction
definition: A reaction in which two or more groups of atoms are removed from the molecule, resulting in a change of skeletal bond order.

label: Bamford-Stevens reaction
definition: The reaction of a tosylhydrazone of an aldehyde or ketone with a strong base to give an olefin.

label: Shapiro reaction
definition: The reaction of a tosylhydrazone of an aldehyde or ketone with an alkyllithium or Grignard reagent to give an olefin.

label: Doebner reaction
definition: The reaction between an aromatic amine, an aldehyde and pyruvic acid on heating to give a quinoline 4-carboxylic acid (cinchoninic acid).

label: Balz-Schiemann reaction
definition: The conversion of an aromatic amine to the corresponding fluoride via the diazonium fluoroborate.

label: Kolbe electrolysis
definition: The decarboxylation and coupling of a carboxylate or mixture of carboxylates to give a dimer.

label: Ugi reaction
definition: Reaction of a ketone or aldehyde, amine, an isocyanide and carboxylic acid to form a bisamide.

label: Wacker-Tsuji oxidation
definition: The oxidation of a terminal alkene or ethene to a methyl ketone or acetaldehyde, catalysed by PdCl2 and CuCl2, and using molecular oxygen as the oxidant.

label: Wacker process
definition: The oxidation of ethene to acetaldehyde, catalysed by PdCl2 and CuCl2, and using molecular oxygen as the oxidant.

label: Monsanto process
definition: The reaction of carbon monoxide with methanol to form acetic acid, catalysed by a rhodium complex.

label: Cativa process
definition: The reaction of carbon monoxide with methanol to form acetic acid, catalysed by an iridium complex.

label: Barton-McCombie deoxygenation
definition: The deoxygenation of an alcohol to the corresponding alkane using a thiochloroformate. It proceeds by a radical mechanism.

label: Barton decarboxylation
definition: The decarboxylation of a carboxylic acid to the lower homologue alkane using a pyridine thione.

label: Rosenmund reduction
definition: The hydrogenolysis of an acyl halide to the corresponding aldehyde, catalysed by palladium-on-barium sulfate.

label: Sonogashira coupling
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where a terminal alkyne reacts with an aryl halide to give an aryl alkyne. It is catalysed by palladium.

label: benzoin condensation
definition: A reaction between two aromatic aldehydes to give a benzoin (an alpha-hydroxyketone).

label: Gattermann reaction
definition: The formylation of an arene with hydrogen cyanide to give an aryl aldehyde or ketone.

label: Suzuki-Miyaura coupling
definition: The coupling of a boronic acid or boronic ester with an unsaturated halide, triflate or sulfonate catalysed by a palladium(0) complex.

label: Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation
definition: The epoxidation of an allylic alcohol to give a beta,gamma-epoxy alcohol, with control of absolute stereochemistry at the two new stereocentres. The oxidising agent is tert-butyl hydroperoxide, and the catalyst is a complex of titanium salt and optically active diethyl tartrate.

label: Sharpless asymmetric dihydroxylation
definition: The dihydroxylation of an alkene to give an alpha,beta-diol, with control of absolute stereochemistry at the two new stereocentres. The oxidising agent is osmium tetroxide, which is used catalytically with a stoichiometric oxidant and a chiral ligand. The reagents are available commercially as AD-mix.

label: Sharpless asymmetric aminohydroxylation
definition: The aminohydroxylation of an alkene to give a beta-amino alcohol, with control of absolute stereochemistry at the two new stereocentres. The oxidising agent is an oxygen-containing osmium species and/or an amino derivative which can be used catalytically with a stoichiometric oxidant and a chiral ligand.

label: Kumada coupling
definition: A cross-coupling reaction between a Grignard reagent and an activated (aryl or vinyl) halide or triflate. The reaction is catalysed by a palladium or nickel catalyst.

label: Barbier-Wieland degradation
definition: The degradation of a carboxylic acid to its next lower homologous carboxylic acid.

label: Corey-Fuchs reaction
definition: A homologation reaction of an aldehyde to yield a terminal alkyne. The reagents are tetrabromomethane, triphenylphosphine, butyllithium and water.

label: Emde degradation
definition: A reduction of a quaternary ammonium salt containing at least one unsaturated substituent, to give a tertiary amine.

label: Claisen rearrangement
definition: The [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of an allyl vinyl ether to give a gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compound.

label: Ireland-Claisen rearrangement
definition: The enolisation and subsequent [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of an allylic acetate to give a gamma,delta-unsaturated carboxylic acid.

label: joining with rearrangement
definition: A reaction in which one or more atoms are added to the molecular skeleton and a skeletal rearrangement occurs.

label: Bellus-Claisen rearrangement
definition: The coupling and subsequent [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of an allylic ether or allylic thioether or allylic amine and a ketene to give the corresponding gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compound.

label: Eschenmoser-Claisen rearrangement
definition: The coupling and subsequent [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of an allylic alcohol and alpha,alpha-dialkoxy ethylamine to give a gamma,delta-unsaturated dialkyl amide.

label: Johnson-Claisen rearrangement
definition: The coupling and subsequent [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of an allylic alcohol and trimethyl orthoacetate to give a gamma,delta-unsaturated alkyl ester.

label: Swern oxidation
definition: The oxidation of a primary alcohol or secondary alcohol to an aldehyde or ketone respectively. Does not work with allylic or benzylic alcohols.

label: Finkelstein reaction
definition: The conversion of an alkyl chloride, alkyl bromide or alkyl sulfonate ester to an alkyl iodide by SN2 substitution. The reaction relies upon the equilibrium being pushed to completion by the precipitation.

label: Lossen rearrangement
definition: The derivatisation (for example with tosyl chloride) and subsequent rearrangement of a hydroxamic acid to give an isocyanate.

label: Nef reaction
definition: The conversion of a primary nitroalkane or secondary nitroalkane to an aldehyde or ketone, respectively.

label: Japp-Klingemann reaction
definition: The reaction of a doubly activated methylene compound that has at least one carbonyl substituent with an aryl azide to give a hydrazone. Usually there is concomitant cleaving and oxidation of one of the carbonyl substituents.

label: Doebner-Miller reaction
definition: The formation of a quinoline from a primary aromatic amine and an alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compound.

label: Fmoc protection
definition: The protection of an amine (often a peptide) as its 9H-fluoren-9-ylmethoxycarbonyl derivative.

label: Paal-Knorr synthesis
definition: The cyclisation of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound with another molecule to give a five-membered aromatic ring compound.

label: Paal-Knorr furan synthesis
definition: The cyclisation of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound with an acid catalyst to give a furan.

label: Paal-Knorr thiophene synthesis
definition: The cyclisation of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound with phosphorus pentasulfide to give a thiophene.

label: Paal-Knorr pyrrole synthesis
definition: The cyclisation of a 1,4-dicarbonyl compound with an amine to give a pyrrole.

label: Schotten-Baumann reaction
definition: The coupling of an alcohol or amine with an acyl chloride to give an ester or amide. A two-phase solvent system is often used, to enable the acid generated during the reaction to be neutralised by the aqueous base, while the starting materials and products remain in the organic phase.

label: Hofmann elimination
definition: The elimination of a methylated amine from an alkylamine to form an olefin. The reaction proceeds via the quaternary ammonium salt.

label: Fischer-Speier esterification
definition: The esterification of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol. Various Lewis acids or Bronsted acids may be used as catalyst.

label: Weerman reaction
definition: The degradation of an alpha-hydroxy amide or an alpha,beta-unsaturated amide to the lower homologue aldehyde, especially in carbohydrate chemistry. The process proceeds via Hofmann degradation.

label: Dakin reaction
definition: The reaction of ortho- or para-phenolic (or ortho- or para-anilinic) aldehydes or ketones with hydrogen peroxide to form bisphenols (or hydroxy anilines) and carboxylic acids.

label: Schmidt rearrangement, carboxylic acid
definition: The reaction of a carboxylic acid with hydrazoic acid to give the lower homologue amine, with the loss of the terminal carbon atom.

label: Schmidt rearrangement, ketone
definition: The reaction of a ketone with hydrazoic acid with the insertion of an NH group between the carbonyl carbon and one of the R groups to form an amide.

label: Schmidt rearrangement, tertiary alcohol
definition: The reaction of a tertiary alcohol with hydrazoic acid with rearrangement to form an imide.

label: Schmidt rearrangement, alkene
definition: The reaction of an alkene with hydrazoic acid with rearrangement to form an imide.

label: Sommelet-Hauser rearrangement
definition: The [2,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of a benzyl quaternary ammonium salt to form an aniline.

label: Dakin-West reaction
definition: The reaction of an amino acid with an acid anhydride to give an alpha-acylamido ketone. The reaction proceeds via the mixed anhydride and an oxazolone; the product is always racemic.

label: Beckmann fragmentation
definition: The cleavage of an oxime to the corresponding nitrile and an alkyl halide. This reaction is related to the Beckmann rearrangement, and occurs when the R group that is anti to the oxime hydroxyl is quaternary.

label: Kharasch reaction
definition: The transfer of a hydrogen or halogen atom from a haloalkane to an alkene, giving the chain-extended halogenated alkane.

label: Kharasch-Sosnovsky reaction
definition: The allylic oxidation of an alkene with a peroxy ester or a peroxide to give the corresponding allylic alcohol derivative. The catalyst is a copper compound.

label: Elbs persulfate oxidation, phenol variant
definition: The oxidation of a phenol to give a (predominantly para) diphenol using potassium persulfate.

label: Elbs persulfate oxidation, tolyl variant
definition: The oxidation of a methyl-substituted aromatic compound to the corresponding benzaldehyde using potassium persulfate.

label: Boyland-Sims oxidation
definition: The oxidation of an aromatic amine to give a (predominantly ortho) amino aryl sulfate using potassium persulfate followed by hydrolysis to yield the corresponding aminophenol.

label: Kolbe-Schmitt reaction
definition: The (predominantly ortho) carboxylation of a phenoxide.

label: Perkow reaction
definition: The reaction between an alpha-halocarbonyl compound and a trialkyl phosphite to give an enol phosphate.

label: Pfitzner-Moffatt oxidation
definition: The oxidation of a primary or secondary alcohol to the corresponding aldehyde or ketone respectively using dimethyl sulfoxide, 1,3-dicyclohexylcarbodiimide and acid. The reaction proceeds via an alkoxysulfonium ylide.

label: Willgerodt reaction
definition: The conversion of an aryl alkyl ketone to an amide and/or an ammonium salt of a carboxylic acid using ammonium polysulfide. The carbonyl group of the product is always at the end of the alkyl chain.

label: Willgerodt-Kindler reaction
definition: The conversion of an aryl alkyl ketone to an amide using sulfur and dry ammonia or a primary or secondary amine. The amide carbonyl group is always at the end of the alkyl chain.

label: Tishchenko reaction
definition: The formation of an ester from two aldehydes via a disproportionation process using a metal alkoxide to oxidise one aldehyde and reduce the other.

label: Zimmermann reaction
definition: The reaction between a methylene ketone and a polynitro aromatic compound.

label: Neber rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of the sulfonic ester of a ketoxime in base to give an alpha-amino ketone.

label: Nozaki-Hiyama-Kishi reaction
definition: A coupling reaction of an aldehyde to an allyl, vinyl or aryl halide or triflate in the presence of chromium(II). It is catalysed by nickel.

label: Buchwald-Hartwig amination
definition: A reaction in which an aryl halide or aryl triflate reacts with a primary or secondary amine in the presence of base to give a secondary or tertiary amine. It is catalysed by a palladium catalyst.

label: Hiyama coupling
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an organosilane reacts with an organic halide or triflate. It is catalysed by a palladium or nickel complex.

label: Stevens rearrangement
definition: The 1,2-rearrangement of an alpha-methylene ammonium salt or alpha-methylene sulfonium salt to an amine or sulfide in the presence of strong base.

label: Stevens rearrangement, ammonium
definition: The 1,2-rearrangement of an alpha-methylene ammonium salt to an amine in the presence of strong base.

label: Stevens rearrangement, sulfonium
definition: The 1,2-rearrangement of an alpha-methylene sulfonium salt to a sulfide in the presence of strong base.

label: aza-Cope rearrangement
definition: A [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement where an aza-1,5-hexadiene rearranges to form an aza-1,5-hexadiene.

label: 2-aza-Cope rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of a 2-aza-1,5-hexadiene to form an imine.

label: 3-aza-Cope rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of a 3-aza-1,5-hexadiene to form a delta,epsilon-unsaturated imine.

label: 1-aza-Cope rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of a delta,epsilon-unsaturated imine to form a 3-aza-1,5-hexadiene.

label: Steglich rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of an oxazolyl carbonate to a C-carboxyazlactone.

label: ring rearrangement
definition: A reaction in which the number of rings in a molecule stays the same but the connectivity of the atoms in at least one ring changes.

label: deprotection reaction
definition: A reaction in which a protecting group is modified by converting it into a functional group.

label: multiple-bond metathesis
definition: A joining reaction in which two carbon-carbon multiple bonds are redistributed among two fragments. The reaction is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutanes). The reaction is driven by release of a low-MW alkene.

label: ring breaking
definition: A planned reaction step in which the product has fewer rings than the reactants.

label: ring contraction
definition: A planned reaction step in which an existing ring is reduced in size.

label: Strecker reaction
definition: An addition reaction between a carbonyl compound, an inorganic cyanide and an ammonium salt, followed by hydrolysis, to give an alpha-amino carboxylic acid.

label: aromatic Claisen rearrangement
definition: The [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement, and subsequent re-aromatisation, of an allyl aryl ether to give an ortho-allyl phenol.

label: Nazarov cyclisation
definition: The acid-catalysed cyclisation of a divinylketone to give a cyclopentenone.

label: Fleming-Tamao oxidation
definition: The stereospecific oxidation of a (phenyldimethylsilyl)alkane to the corresponding alcohol using bromine, usually generated in situ from potassium bromide and peracetic acid.

label: Tamao oxidation
definition: The stereospecific oxidation of a (halosilyl)alkane to the corresponding alcohol using potassium fluoride and hydrogen peroxide.

label: Sakurai reaction, aldehyde or ketone
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an aldehyde or ketone reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a homoallylic alcohol.

label: Sakurai reaction, enone
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an enone reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a delta,epsilon-unsaturated carbonyl compound.

label: Sakurai reaction, acid chloride
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an acid chloride reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a gamma,delta-unsaturated ketone.

label: Sakurai reaction, acetal or ketal
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an acetal or ketal reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a homoallylic ether.

label: imine Sakurai reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an imine reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a homoallylic amine.

label: Sakurai reaction, epoxide
definition: A ring-breaking reaction in which an epoxide reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a hydroxy alkene.

label: Cannizzaro reaction
definition: The disproportionation of a non-enolisable aldehyde to the corresponding carboxylic acid and alcohol. The reaction is base-catalysed.

label: crossed Cannizzaro reaction
definition: The reaction between two different non-enolisable aldehydes to give a carboxylic acid and an alcohol. The reaction is base-catalysed.

label: Pummerer rearrangement
definition: The conversion of a sulfoxide with an alpha-hydrogen to an alpha-substituted thioether. The reaction is usually performed with an acid anhydride or acyl halide.

label: Polonovski reaction
definition: The reaction of a tertiary amine oxide with an anhydride or acyl chloride to give an N,N-disubstituted acetamide and an aldehyde.

label: Polonovski-Potier reaction
definition: The reaction of a tertiary amine oxide with trifluoroacetic anhydride to give an N,N-disubstituted acetamide and an aldehyde.

label: Menshutkin reaction
definition: The alkylation of a tertiary amine with an alkyl halide to the corresponding ammonium salt.

label: Strecker degradation
definition: The deamination and decarboxylation of amino acids to imines and then to aldehydes or ketones, in the presence of a carbonyl compound (especially an alpha,beta-dicarbonyl compound or a reducing sugar such as glucose). The reaction is involved in the generation of meat aromas/flavours in cooking.

label: Wohl-Ziegler bromination
definition: The allylic or benzylic bromination of hydrocarbons using an N-bromoimide and a radical initiator, to give an allylic or benzylic bromide.

label: Wolff-Kishner reduction
definition: The reduction of an aldehyde or ketone to the corresponding methylene compound, using hydrazine hydrate and a base.

label: Wolff-Kishner reduction, Huang-Minlon modification
definition: The reduction of an aldehyde or ketone to the corresponding methylene compound, using hydrazine hydrate and a base in refluxing ethylene glycol.

label: Zemplen transesterification
definition: The reduction of an ester to the corresponding alcohol, using sodium methoxide in dry methanol.

label: Ferrier rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of an O-alkenyl acetal to give the corresponding beta-alkoxy carbonyl compound.

label: aza-Ferrier rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of an O-alkenyl hemiaminal ether to give the corresponding beta-amino carbonyl compound.

label: aza-ene reaction
definition: A joining reaction where an N=C system with an allylic hydrogen reacts with a compound containing a multiple bond (an enophile) to form a substituted alkene.

label: Petasis reaction
definition: The alkylation of an amine with a boronic acid and an aldehyde to give a substituted amine.

label: Achmatowicz rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a furfuryl alcohol derivative to a dihydropyranone, from which monosaccharides may be synthesised.

label: aza-Achmatowicz rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a furfuryl amine derivative to a tetrahydropyridinone.

label: aza-Henry reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a nitroalkane and an imine to give a beta-amino nitroalkane.

label: Biginelli reaction
definition: A three-component coupling of a beta-keto ester, an aldehyde and urea to give a 3,4-dihydropyrimidinone.

label: Blaise reaction
definition: The coupling of an alpha-bromo ester with a nitrile to give a beta-ketoester, in the presence of zinc.

label: Katritzky pyrylium-pyridinium method
definition: The substitution of an amine with a nucleophile using a pyrylium salt.

label: metalla-aza-Claisen rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of a 3-aza-4-metalla-1,5-hexadiene to form a delta,epsilon-unsaturated metallaimine.

label: Bergman cyclisation
definition: The cyclisation of an ene-diyne to give a 1,4-diradical cyclohexatriene. The diradical usually aromatises.

label: nitro-Michael reaction
definition: A conjugate addition reaction where a carbon nucleophile adds to an alpha,beta-unsaturated nitro compound.

label: Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation, alkene
definition: The homogeneous asymmetric catalytic hydrogenation of a C=C double bond using a ruthenium(II) BINAP catalyst.

label: Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation, carbonyl
definition: The homogeneous asymmetric catalytic hydrogenation of a carbonyl bond using a ruthenium(II) BINAP catalyst.

label: Passerini reaction
definition: The reaction between an isonitrile, a carboxylic acid and aldehyde or ketone to give an alpha-acyloxy amide.

label: ring-closing metathesis
definition: A joining reaction in which two carbon-carbon double bonds in the same molecule are redistributed to give a cycloalkene. It is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutanes). The reaction is driven by release of a low-MW alkene.

label: polymerisation reaction
definition: A joining reaction, the objective of which is that a large number of monomers react together to produce a polymer consisting of repeating units.

label: ring-opening metathesis polymerisation
definition: A joining reaction in which two carbon-carbon double bonds are redistributed among two fragments, i.e. two alkenes. It is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutanes). The reaction is driven by release of ring strain in the cycloalkene.

label: Carroll rearrangement
definition: An adaptation of the Claisen rearrangement that transforms a beta-keto allyl ester into a gamma,delta-allyl ketone. It uses base and high temperature or milder conditions with a ruthenium or palladium complex as catalyst.

label: Bose-Mitsunobu reaction
definition: A Mitsunobu reaction, where an alcohol is converted to something else with inversion at the stereocentre, using diphenylphosphoryl azide.

label: Fritsch-Buttenberg-Wiechell rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of a 2-bromoalkene into an alkyne, mediated by strong base.

label: Stork-Wittig olefination
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an aldehyde reacts with a phosphonium salt to give a Z-iodoalkene. The reaction is mediated by sodium or potassium hexamethyldisilazide.

label: Cadogan carbazole synthesis
definition: The reduction of a 2-nitrobiaryl compound to give a carbazole.

label: Cadogan coupling
definition: The arylation of an arylamine mediated by isopentyl nitrite.

label: MacDonald condensation
definition: The reaction between a 1,9-diformyldipyrromethane and a dipyrromethane to give a porphodimethene.

label: Zweifel diene synthesis
definition: The carbon-carbon homocoupling reaction of a pair of alkyne molecules using a borane and iodine. The reaction proceeds via a borane intermediate.

label: Dess-Martin oxidation
definition: The oxidation of a primary alcohol to an aldehyde or a secondary alcohol to a ketone using the Dess-Martin periodinane.

label: Criegee oxidation
definition: The oxidative cleavage of an alpha,beta-diol using lead tetraacetate to give the corresponding carbonyl compounds.

label: Simmons-Smith reaction
definition: A ring formation reaction where an alkene reacts with a dihalomethane in the presence of a metal atom or metal complexes to form a cyclopropane. The traditional reaction uses a zinc-copper couple and diiodomethane.

label: Wittig rearrangement
definition: Any of a family of sigmatropic rearrangements that involve the migration of a group on an electronegative atom (O or N) to a carbon atom.

label: [1,2]-Wittig rearrangement
definition: A base-mediated 1,2-sigmatropic rearrangement of an ether to give an alcohol.

label: [2,3]-Wittig rearrangement
definition: A base-mediated 2,3-sigmatropic rearrangement of an allyl ether to give a homoallylic alcohol.

label: [1,2]-aza-Wittig rearrangement
definition: A base-mediated 1,2-sigmatropic rearrangement of a tertiary amine to give a secondary amine.

label: [2,3]-aza-Wittig rearrangement
definition: A base-mediated 2,3-sigmatropic rearrangement of an activated tertiary allyl amine, to give a secondary homoallylic amine.

label: aza-Baylis-Hillman reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling of an imine to an activated alkene in the presence of a nucleophilic base to give an allylic amine.

label: aza-Wittig reaction
definition: Reaction of a carbonyl compound with a phosphazene to give an imine.

label: Fenton reaction
definition: An oxidation reaction involving Fenton's reagent.

label: Hantzsch pyridine synthesis
definition: A ring-forming reaction between two equivalents of a beta-ketoester, an aldehyde and ammonia (or an ammonium salt), to give a dihydropyridine. The dihydropyridine can be oxidised to a pyridine using an oxidising agent.

label: alkyne-azide click reaction
definition: A [3+2] cycloaddition reaction between an azide and an alkyne to give a 1,2,3-triazole, needing only heat to proceed.

label: copper(I)-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition
definition: A [3+2] cycloaddition reaction between an azide and an alkyne to give a 1,2,3-triazole as a single regioisomer.

label: Fischer-Tropsch process
definition: A process in which carbon monoxide and hydrogen ('syngas') are converted into various liquid hydrocarbons.

label: hydroformylation
definition: The reaction of hydrogen and carbon monoxide with an alkene, to give an aldehyde, catalysed by a rhodium or cobalt catalyst. The process is equivalent to the formal addition of the H-C bond of formaldehyde across the double bond of an alkene.

label: retro-Diels-Alder reaction
definition: The reverse of a [4+2] cycloaddition reaction, in which an unsaturated six-membered ring compound fragments to give a diene and a compound containing a double or triple bond (a dienophile).

label: retro-aldol addition
definition: The reverse of an aldol addition, in which a beta-hydroxy ketone fragments to form a carbonyl compound and the enolate of an alpha-acidic carbonyl compound.

label: Stork-Crabtree hydrogenation
definition: A hydrogenation in which a metal-ligating group distant from the reacting alkene directs the attack of hydrogen, giving preferentially one stereoisomer of the product.

label: Aufbau reaction
definition: The insertion of alkenes (usually ethene) into the Al-C bond of an aluminium alkyl, to give a long-chain aluminium alkyl that can go on to react again, or can decompose into the corresponding terminal alkene. The addition of transition metals results in reliable production of high molecular weight polymers.

label: hydroalumination
definition: The reaction of an aluminium alkyl with an alkene, to give an intermediate aluminium species that can give various products after work-up.

label: hydrovinylation
definition: The reaction of two alkenes to give an elongated alkene, employing a transition-metal-based catalyst.

label: 1,4-hydrovinylation
definition: The reaction of a 1,3-diene with an alkene to give a 1,4-diene, usually employing a cobalt-based catalyst (see Hilt and Luers, Synthesis, 2002, 609).

label: alkene metathesis
definition: A joining reaction in which two carbon-carbon double bonds are redistributed among two fragments, i.e. two alkenes. It is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutanes). The reaction is driven by release of a low-MW alkene.

label: alkyne metathesis
definition: A joining reaction in which two carbon-carbon triple bonds are redistributed among two fragments, i.e. two alkynes. It is catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutenes). The reaction is driven by release of a low-MW alkyne.

label: enyne metathesis
definition: A joining reaction in which a carbon-carbon double bond and a carbon-carbon triple bond are redistributed among two fragments, i.e. an alkene and an alkyne. It is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutenes). As a low-MW alkene is not released, the reaction is usually driven by formation of a ring.

label: ring-closing enyne metathesis
definition: A joining reaction in which a carbon-carbon double bond and a carbon-carbon triple bond in the same molecule are redistributed to give a alkene-substituted cycloalkene. It is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutanes). The reaction is driven by formation of the ring.

label: ring-opening metathesis
definition: A ring-breaking reaction in which two carbon-carbon double bonds are redistributed among two fragments, i.e. an alkene and a cycloalkene. It is typically catalysed by a metal carbenoid, and proceeds via two four-membered intermediates (metallacyclobutanes). The reaction is driven by release of ring strain in the cycloalkene.

label: Luche reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an aldehyde or ketone reacts with an allyl halide and zinc to give a homoallylic alcohol.

label: Luche reduction
definition: The chemoselective reduction of an enone to give an allylic alcohol, using sodium borohydride and an lanthanoid chloride, typically cerium chloride.

label: Lindgren oxidation
definition: The oxidation of an aldehyde to a carboxylic acid with chlorite.

label: Rosenmund-von Braun reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an aryl halide reacts with copper(I) cyanide to give an aryl nitrile.

label: Woehler reaction
definition: The formation of urea from ammonium cyanate. Ammonium cyanate is unstable, and so is formed in situ from either (a) cyanic acid and ammonia, (b) a metal cyanate and an ammonium salt, or (c) a metal cyanate and ammonia.

label: hydrosilylation
definition: The addition of a hydrosilane across the double bond of an alkene or alkyne to give a more substituted silane. Addition to an aldehyde or ketone produces a silyl ether.

label: hydroamination
definition: The addition of the N-H bond of an amine across a double bond of an alkene or alkyne, to give a more substituted amine.

label: hydroboration
definition: The addition of a boron hydride across a double bond of an alkene or alkyne. The boron usually adds to the less substituted carbon.

label: hydroboration-oxidation
definition: The formal addition of water across a double bond of an alkene or alkyne by formation of an organoborane and in situ oxidation, typically with basic peroxide. The product is usually the less substituted alcohol.

label: hydrometallation
definition: The reaction of any of a number of metal hydride species with an alkene or alkyne, to give an intermediate organometal species that can give various products after work-up.

label: hydrozirconation
definition: The reaction of a organozirconium hydride with an alkene or alkyne, to give an intermediate zirconium species that can give various products after work-up. The zirconium reagent used is typically Schwartz's reagent, Cp2ZrHCl.

label: hydrostannylation
definition: The reaction of a organotin hydride with an alkene or alkyne, to give an intermediate tin species that can give various products after work-up.

label: hydrotelluration
definition: The reaction of a organotellurium hydride with an alkyne, to give a (usually (Z)-configured) vinyltellurium species.

label: Fetizon oxidation
definition: The oxidation of a primary alcohol to an aldehyde or a secondary alcohol to a ketone using Fetizon's reagent.

label: Vorbrueggen condensation
definition: The substitution of an acylated sugar with a silylated base to give a nucleoside.

label: thiol-ene reaction
definition: The addition of a thiol across the double bond of an alkene to give a thioether.

label: Yamamoto polymerisation
definition: The nickel-catalysed carbon-carbon coupling of a dihaloaromatic compound or aromatic bistriflate to give a polymer.

label: thermal hydrosilylation
definition: The thermally induced addition of a hydrosilane across the double bond of an alkene or alkyne to give a more substituted silane, usually applied to cases where the hydrosilane is part of a silicon surface.

label: photohydrosilylation
definition: The photochemically induced addition of a hydrosilane across the double bond of an alkene or alkyne to give a more substituted silane, usually applied to cases where the hydrosilane is part of a silicon surface. The reaction uses UV irradiation and is usually initiated by an added photoactive compound such as a platinum catalyst.

label: Julia-Kocienski olefination
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a tetrazole sulfone and an aldehyde or ketone to give a (typically trans) alkene.

label: seleno-Pummerer rearrangement
definition: The conversion of a selenoxide with an alpha-hydrogen to an alpha-subtituted selenoether.

label: intramolecular Heck reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an organohalide or triflate couples to an alkene in the same molecule, catalysed by palladium.

label: Mori-Ban indole synthesis
definition: An intramolecular Heck reaction of an ortho-halo-aniline with an alkene, catalysed by palladium.

label: Yamaguchi lactonisation
definition: The intramolecular esterification of a carboxylic acid group with an alcohol group using 2,4,6-trichlorobenzoyl chloride (Yamaguchi reagent) - an intramolecular Yamaguchi esterification.

label: Yamaguchi esterification
definition: The esterification of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol using 2,4,6-trichlorobenzoyl chloride (Yamaguchi reagent).

label: oxa-Diels-Alder reaction
definition: A Diels-Alder reaction between a diene and an aldehyde or ketone to give a six-membered oxygen-containing ring compound.

label: Tsuji-Trost reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which a nucleophile reacts with an allyl acetate, allyl halide, allyl carbonate, allyl carbamate, allyl phosphate, allyl sulfone or allyl nitro compound to give an allyl compound. The reaction is catalsed by Pd(0).

label: Clauson-Kaas reaction
definition: A ring rearrangement reaction where an amine reacts with a 2,5-dialkoxytetrahydrofuran to give a pyrrole.

label: hetero-Diels-Alder reaction
definition: A [4+2] cycloaddition reaction where a diene (or heterocyclic analogue) reacts with a dienophile to form an unsaturated heterocyclic six-membered ring compound.

label: palladium-catalysed carbon-carbon coupling reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction that is catalysed by a palladium atom.

label: ester condensation reaction
definition: A condensation reaction that results in the production of an ester.

label: amino group deprotection
definition: A deprotection reaction where the reactive centre is an amino nitrogen atom.

label: amino group protection
definition: A protection reaction where the reactive centre is an amino nitrogen atom.

label: benzylamine protection
definition: An amino group protection where the amino nitrogen atom is protected by reaction with benzylamine.

label: hydroxyl group protection
definition: A protection reaction where the reactive centre is a hydroxyl oxygen atom.

label: benzyl ether protection
definition: A hydroxyl group protection where the hydroxyl oxygen atom is protected by formation of a benzyl ether.

label: carboxylic acid protection
definition: A protection reaction where the reactive centre is a carboxy oxygen.

label: Boc deprotection
definition: An amino group deprotection reaction where a tertbutoxycarbonyl group is removed in acid conditions.

label: benzylamine deprotection
definition: An amino group deprotection reaction where a benzylamine group is removed by hydrogenation.

label: silyl protection
definition: A hydroxyl group protection where the hydroxyl oxygen atom is protected by formation of a silyl ether.

label: planned synthesis
definition: An experimental procedure with the aim of producing a portion of a given compound or mixture.

label: acetate protection
definition: A hydroxyl group protection where the hydroxyl oxygen atom is protected by formation of an acetate ester.

label: substitution step
definition: A functional modification step in which one singly-bonded substituent, but not a hydrogen, is replaced by another singly-bonded substituent.

label: aromatic substitution step
definition: A substitution step where one singly-bonded substituent on an aromatic skeleton is replaced by another singly-bonded substituent.

label: Sandmeyer halogenation
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(I) halide to form an aromatic halide.

label: Sandmeyer pseudohalogenation
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(II) pseudohalide to form an aromatic pseudohalide.

label: reductive amination
definition: A functional group modification where a carbonyl oxygen is replaced by an amine.

label: nitro reduction to amine
definition: A reduction reaction where a nitro group is reduced to an amino group.

label: amide reduction to amine
definition: A reduction process where an amide is transformed into an amine.

label: alkene reduction to alkane
definition: A hydrogenation reaction where an alkene is reduced to an alkane.

label: amide N-alkylation
definition: An N-alkylation where the reactive centre is an amide nitrogen.

label: aniline N-alkylation
definition: An N-alkylation where the reactive centre is an aniline nitrogen.

label: nitrile reduction to amine
definition: A reduction where a nitrile is replaced by an amine function.

label: imine reduction to amine
definition: A reduction reaction where an imine function is replaced by an amine.

label: alkene oxidative cleavage
definition: An alkene oxidation where the carbon-carbon double bond is cleaved.

label: heteroaryl N-alkylation
definition: An N-alkylation reaction where the reactive centre is an azacycle ring nitrogen.

label: oxacycle synthesis
definition: A ring formation reaction where the intended product contains an oxacycle that was not in any of the reactants.

label: azacycle synthesis
definition: A ring formation reaction where the intended product contains an azacycle that was not in any of the reactants.

label: thiacycle synthesis
definition: A ring formation reaction where the intended product contains an thiacycle that was not in any of the reactants.

label: heterocycle synthesis
definition: A ring formation reaction where the intended product contains a heterocycle that was not in any of the reactants.

label: alkyne reduction to alkane
definition: A hydrogenation reaction where an alkyne is reduced to an alkane.

label: aryl reduction to cycloalkyl
definition: A hydrogenation reaction where an aryl ring is reduced to a cycloalkyl ring.

label: heteroaryl reduction to saturated heterocycle
definition: A hydrogenation reaction where a heteroaryl is reduced to a saturated heterocycle.

label: alcohol oxidation to carboxylic acid
definition: An alcohol oxidation where the alcohol centre is replaced with a carboxy group.

label: benzylic oxidation
definition: An oxidation reaction where a carbon centre bonded to the benzene ring is oxidized to a carboxyl group.

label: allylic oxidation
definition: An oxidation reaction where an allylic C-H bond is replaced by a C=O bond.

label: Jones oxidation
definition: An oxidation reaction where an alcohol is oxidized to the corresponding carbonyl compound by a dichromate ion.

label: N-acylation to amide
definition: An acylation reaction where a nitrogen atom is acylated to form an amide.

label: N-acylation to urea
definition: An acylation reaction where a nitrogen atom is acylated to form a urea.

label: N-acylation to carbamate
definition: An N-acylation reaction where a nitrogen centre is acylated to form a carbamate.

label: O-acylation to ester
definition: An O-acylation reaction where an oxygen centre is acylated to result in an ester.

label: Pinner reaction
definition: A functional group modification where a nitrile reacts with an alcohol under acid catalysis to form an amidine or ester.

label: nitrile alcoholysis
definition: An oxidation reaction where a nitrile reacts with an alcohol to form an alkyl imidate.

label: amide to imidoyl chloride conversion
definition: A functional group modification where an amide is converted to an imidoyl chloride.

label: alcohol to halide conversion
definition: A functional group modification where an alcohol is converted to a halide.

label: carboxylic acid to acid chloride conversion
definition: A functional group modification where a carboxylic acid is converted to an acid chloride.

label: nitrile to acid conversion
definition: A functional group modification where a nitrile is transformed into a carboxylic acid.

label: carbonyl compound to imine conversion
definition: A functional group modification where a carbonyl compound is converted to an imine.

label: Crabbé homologation
definition: Oxidation of an alkene to an allene by heating with formaldehyde, an amine and an inorganic salt.

label: Friedel-Crafts reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between an arene and a carbonyl compound (an acylation) or an alkyl compound that can form a carbocation (an alkylation) to form a substituted arene.

label: Demko-Sharpless reaction
definition: A functional group modification of a nitrile to a 1H-tetrazole by addition of sodium azide.

label: Griess reaction
definition: A diazotisation that proceeds by the addition of nitrous acid, nitric acid and water, resulting in a diazonium nitrate salt.

label: heteroaryl N-arylation
definition: An N-arylation reaction where the reactive centre is an azacycle ring nitrogen.

label: functional group addition step
definition: A planned reaction step in which a covalent bond is formed between the molecular skeleton and a functional group and where the skeletal bond order is unchanged.

label: Chan-Lam coupling
definition: Arylation of an amine or an alcohol with an arylboronic acid, stannane or siloxane in air with a copper acetate catalyst.

label: Eschweiler-Clarke methylation
definition: N-methylation of a primary or secondary amine with formic acid and formaldehyde to yield a tertiary amine.

label: Scholl reaction
definition: Coupling of two aromatic rings in the presence of a Lewis acid catalyst.

label: intramolecular Scholl reaction
definition: Scholl reaction within a single molecule to form a larger ring system.

label: intermolecular Scholl reaction
definition: Scholl reaction between two aromatic molecules to form a single molecule.

label: Robinson annulation
definition: Reaction of a cyclohexanone with methyl vinyl ketone to form a fused-ring cyclohexenone. It is a Michael reaction followed by an intramolecular aldol condensation.

label: Tiffeneau-Demjanov rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of a beta-amino alcohol to yield a carbonyl compound. In the first step the amine is diazotised by reaction with nitrous acid.

label: Demjanov rearrangement
definition: Substitution reaction of a primary amine via deazotisation to yield a mixture of the corresponding primary alcohol and secondary alcohol.

label: thiol-yne reaction
definition: Reaction of an thiol with an alkyne to form an alkenyl sulfide.

label: cyclic alpha-haloketone Favorskii rearrangement
definition: Base-catalysed rearrangement of a cyclic alpha-haloketone to the ring-contracted cyclic carboxylic acid.

label: Favorskii rearrangement
definition: Base-catalysed rearrangement of an alpha-haloketone to a carboxylic acid or ester or amide.

label: Wallach degradation
definition: Favorskii rearrangement of a cyclic alpha-dibromoketone followed by decarboxylation to yield a ring-contracted cyclic ketone.

label: Seyferth-Gilbert homologation
definition: Reaction of a carbonyl compound with a dimethyl diazomethyl phosphonate under basic conditions to yield an alkyne.

label: Seyferth-Gilbert aldehyde homologation
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde with a dimethyl diazomethyl phosphonate under basic conditions to yield a terminal alkyne.

label: Seyferth-Gilbert ketone homologation
definition: Reaction of a ketone with a dimethyl diazomethyl phosphonate under basic conditions to yield a terminal alkyne.

label: Seyferth-Gilbert-Bestmann homologation
definition: Seyferth-Gilbert aldehyde homologation where the Seyferth-Gilbert reagent is generated in situ by reaction with methanol and potassium carbonate.

label: Knorr pyrazole synthesis
definition: Synthesis of a pyrazole by condensation of a 1,3-dicarbonyl compound with a hydrazine, hydrazide, semicarbazide or aminoguanidine.

label: pyrazole synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is a pyrazole that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: quinoline synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is a quinoline that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: Knorr quinoline synthesis
definition: Synthesis of an alpha-hydroxyquinoline by reaction of a beta-ketoester and an arylamine then dehydration with concentrated sulfuric acid.

label: indole synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is an indole that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: Larock indole synthesis
definition: Palladium-catalysed synthesis of a 2,3-substituted indole by reaction of an ortho-iodoaniline with an internal alkyne.

label: Pechmann pyrazole synthesis
definition: Reaction of an organic diazo compound with an acetylene to yield a pyrazole.

label: benzimidazole synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is a benzimidazole that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: Phillips benzimidazole synthesis
definition: Reaction of an ortho-diaminobenzene with a carboxylic acid under heat to yield a benzimidazole.

label: pyrimidine synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is a pyrimidine that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: triazine synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is a triazine that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: Pinner pyrimidine synthesis
definition: Reaction of a 1,3-dicarbonyl compound with an amidine to yield a pyrimidine.

label: Pinner triazine synthesis
definition: Reaction of aryl amidines or halogenated aliphatics amidines with phosgene to yield a 2-hydroxy-4,6-diaryl-s-triazine with loss of ammonia.

label: oxa-Pictet-Spengler reaction
definition: Cyclisation-condensation of a beta-arylethanol with an aldehyde to yield an ortho,peri-fused aryl-oxacycle.

label: Prilezhaev epoxidation
definition: Epoxidation of an alkene by reaction with a peroxy acid.

label: Appel halogenation
definition: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl halide with triphenylphosphine and a halide source.

label: Appel iodination
definition: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl iodide with triphenylphosphine and tetraiodomethane.

label: Appel chlorination
definition: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl chloride with triphenylphosphine and tetrachloromethane.

label: Appel bromination
definition: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl bromide with triphenylphosphine and tetrabromomethane or bromine.

label: Hofmann rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of a primary carboxylic amide to an isocyanate.

label: Hofmann reaction
definition: Conversion of a primary carboxylic amide to the lower homologue primary amine. The first step is the Hofmann rearrangement

label: Newman-Kwart rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of an O-aryl thiocarbamate to an S-aryl thiocarbamate by migration of the aryl group.

label: Zincke nitration
definition: Substitution of phenol ortho- or para-bromo or iodo groups by a nitro group on treatment with nitrous acid or a nitrite in acetic acid.

label: haloethanoate ester-aldehyde Darzens reaction
definition: A coupling of an aldehyde with an alpha-haloethanoate ester to form the next homologous aldehyde.

label: haloethanoate ester-ketone Darzens reaction
definition: A coupling of a ketone with an alpha-haloethanoate ester to replace the oxo group with a formyl group.

label: phthalimide protection
definition: Protection of a primary amino group by reaction with phthalic anhydride.

label: phthalimide deprotection
definition: Conversion of a phthalimide into the corresponding primary amine.

label: Kröhnke pyridine synthesis
definition: Condensation of a 1,5-diketone with ammonium acetate in acetic acid to form a pyridine.

label: Gattermann chlorination
definition: A Gattermann halogenation where the aryl diazonium salt reacts with copper powder and hydrochloric acid to yield an aromatic chloride.

label: Gattermann bromination
definition: A Gattermann halogenation where the aryl diazonium salt reacts with copper powder and hydrobromic acid to yield an aromatic bromide.

label: aldehyde Barbier reaction
definition: A joining reaction between an aldehyde and an alkyl halide or aryl halide or vinyl halide or allyl halide in the presence of metal to give a secondary alcohol.

label: ketone Barbier reaction
definition: A joining reaction between an ketone and an alkyl halide or aryl halide or vinyl halide or allyl halide in the presence of metal to give a tertiary alcohol.

label: pivaloyl protection
definition: Protection of a hydroxy group by reaction with pivaloyl chloride, resulting in formation of a pivaloyl ester.

label: pivaloyl deprotection
definition: A hydroxy group deprotection where a pivalate group is removed in acid or base conditions.

label: hydroxy group deprotection
definition: A deprotection reaction where the reactive centre is an oxygen atom.

label: carboxylic acid to primary alcohol reduction
definition: A carboxylic acid reduction where the product is the corresponding primary alcohol.

label: tert-butyl protection
definition: A carboxy group protection where the product is a tert-butyl ester.

label: carboxy group deprotection
definition: A deprotection reaction where the reactive centre is a carboxy oxygen atom.

label: tert-butyl ester deprotection
definition: A deprotection reaction where the reactant is a tert-butyl ester and the product is a carboxylic acid.

label: methyl ester deprotection
definition: A deprotection reaction where the reactant is a methyl ester and the product is a carboxylic acid.

label: ethyl ester deprotection
definition: A deprotection reaction where the reactant is an ethyl ester and the product is a carboxylic acid.

label: molecular transformation objective
definition: An objective specification that describes how a molecule's skeleton and substituents are to be transformed by a given experiment.

label: molecular skeleton cleaving objective
definition: A molecular transformation specification that specifies that atoms should be lost from the molecular skeleton.

label: molecular ring formation objective
definition: A molecular transformation specification that specifies that the product should contain a ring that was not in any of the reactants.

label: molecular skeleton joining objective
definition: A molecular transformation specification that specifies that atoms should be added to the molecular skeleton.

label: molecular rearrangement objective
definition: A molecular transformation specification that specifies that the number of rings and number of skeletal atoms should be preserved and that one or more atoms or groups of atoms move from one atom to another.

label: functional modification objective
definition: A molecular transformation specification that specifies that the substituents of a molecule should be changed but the number of atoms in the skeleton and their connectivity, but not necessarily the bond orders, should remain the same.

label: Sakurai reaction
definition: Reaction of allyltrimethylsilane with a carbon electrophile to allylate the carbon electrophile.

label: Fries rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of the acyl group in a phenolate ester to the ortho- and/or para-position. It is catalysed by a Lewis acid.

label: ortho-Fries rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of the acyl group in a phenolate ester to the ortho-position.

label: para-Fries rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of the acyl group in a phenolate ester to the para-position.

label: Duff reaction
definition: Formylation of a phenol or aromatic amine with hexamethylenetetramine in acidic conditions.

label: molecular chain lengthening objective
definition: A molecular skeleton joining adjective that is satisfied by a chain part of a molecular skeleton being lengthened.

label: molecular ring breaking objective
definition: A molecular skeleton transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction where the product has fewer rings than the reactants.

label: molecular ring contraction objective
definition: A molecular skeleton transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction where an existing ring is reduced in size.

label: aldehyde Sakurai reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which an aldehyde reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a homoallylic alcohol.

label: ketone Sakurai reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction in which a ketone reacts with allyltrimethylsilane to form a homoallylic alcohol.

label: functional group modification objective
definition: A molecular skeleton transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a functional group undergoes a change that does not affect the number of atoms in (or connectivity of) the skeleton of the molecule.

label: end-of-chain chain shortening objective
definition: A molecular skeleton transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a single skeletal atom is removed from the end of a chain.

label: functional reduction objective
definition: A functional group modification objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a functional group is reduced.

label: addition objective
definition: A functional modification objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which two or more groups of atoms are added to the molecule, resulting in a change of skeletal bond order.

label: molecular ring expansion objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which an existing skeletal ring is increased in size.

label: molecular ring rearrangement objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which the number of skeletal rings in a molecule stays the same but the connectivity of the atoms in at least one ring changes.

label: acetoacetic ester synthesis
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between a beta-ketoester and an alkyl halide to yield an alpha-alkylated ester.

label: intramolecular acyloin condensation
definition: A reductive coupling reaction between two ester groups in the same molecule to give an acyloin (an alpha-hydroxyketone).

label: intermolecular acyloin condensation
definition: A reductive coupling reaction between two ester groups in different molecules to give an acyloin (an alpha-hydroxyketone).

label: molecular extrusion objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a cleaving reaction in which a skeletal atom or group of atoms is removed from the middle of a molecule.

label: molecular fragmentation objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which the parent molecule breaks up into three or more products.

label: Pauson-Khand reaction
definition: A [2+2+1] cycloaddition between an alkyne, an alkene and carbon monoxide to form an alpha-beta-unsaturated cyclopentenone.

label: fused-ring-system formation objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a fused ring system is present in a product that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: polymerisation objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a large number of monomers react together to product a polymer consisting of repeating units.

label: Algar-Flynn-Oyamada reaction
definition: A fused-ring-system formation reaction where a flavonol is produced by oxidation of a chalcone with alkaline hydrogen peroxide.

label: molecular substitution objective
definition: A molecular transformation objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which one singly-bonded substituent, but not a hydrogen, is replaced by another singly-bonded substituent.

label: Allan-Robinson flavone synthesis
definition: Reaction of ortho-hydroxyaryl ketones with aromatic carboxylic acid anhydrides to yield a flavone.

label: Allan-Robinson isoflavone synthesis
definition: Reaction of ortho-hydroxyaryl ketones with aromatic carboxylic acid anhydrides to yield an isoflavone.

label: Amadori rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of an aldose N-glycoside to a ketose N-glycoside by acid or base catalysis.

label: Arens-van Dorp synthesis
definition: Reaction of a ketone with ethoxyacetylene to form an alkoxyethynyl alcohol.

label: Auwers synthesis
definition: Ring expansion of a 2-bromo-2-(alpha-bromobenzyl)-1-benzofuran to a flavonol in alcoholic alkali.

label: Allan-Robinson reaction
definition: Reaction of ortho-hydroxyaryl ketones with aromatic acid anhydrides to yield a flavone or isoflavone.

label: Meyer-Schuster rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of a propargyl alcohol to an alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compound via an allene intermediate to yield the gamma-substituted product.

label: Rupe rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of a propargyl alcohol to an alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compound via an enyne intermediate to yield the beta-substituted product.

label: Still-Gennari modification
definition: A Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons reaction that uses phosphonates with electron-withdrawing groups and dissociating conditions to produce predominantly Z-olefins.

label: olefination
definition: A molecular skeleton joining reaction where the connection formed is a carbon-carbon double bond.

label: Tebbe olefination
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde or ketone or carboxylic ester or amide with the Tebbe reagent to yield an olefin or an enol ether or an enamine.

label: Myers-Saito cyclisation
definition: Cyclization of a enynyl allene to yield a toluene biradical with radical centres on the methyl group and at the meta position.

label: Schmittel cyclisation
definition: Cyclization of a enynyl allene to yield a 1-methyl-5-methylenecyclopenta-1,3-diene biradical with radical centres on the methyl group and methylene group.

label: Baudisch reaction
definition: Copper-catalysed reaction of benzene with a nitrosylating agent and hydrogen peroxide to form an o-nitrosophenol.

label: deprotection objective
definition: A functional modification objective that is satisfied by deprotecting some functional group.

label: protection objective
definition: A functional modification objective that is satisfied by protecting some functional group.

label: molecular skeleton elimination objective
definition: A functional modification objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which two or more groups of atoms are removed from the molecule, resulting in a change of skeletal bond order.

label: molecular skeleton insertion objective
definition: A molecular skeleton joining objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which one or more atoms are added to the middle of a chain.

label: joining with rearrangement objective
definition: A molecular skeleton joining objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which one or more atoms are added to the molecular skeleton and a skeletal rearrangement occurs.
