label: functional group addition objective
definition: A functional modification objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a covalent bond is formed between the molecular skeleton and a functional group and where the skeletal bond order is unchanged.

label: functional oxidation objective
definition: A functional group modification objective that is satisfied by a reaction in which a functional group is oxidised.

label: interrupted Nazarov cyclisation
definition: Cyclisation of a propargyl alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone where the reactant rearranges to an allenyl vinyl ketone, followed by a Nazarov cyclisation in which a nucleophile 'intercepts' the intermediate carbocation to yield a cyclopentenone substituted at the alpha position opposite the double bond.

label: Bamberger rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of an N-phenylhydroxamine in acid to yield a para-aminophenol.

label: Bart reaction
definition: Reaction of an aromatic diazonium compound with an alkali arsenite in the presence of copper or silver to yield an aromatic arsonic acid.

label: Bartoli indole synthesis
definition: Reaction of an ortho-substituted nitroarene with a vinyl Grignard reagent to yield a 7-substituted indole.

label: Barton olefination
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde or ketone with a thioaldehyde or thioketone via a 1,3,4-thiadiazoline intermediate to yield an olefin.

label: Barton-Zard reaction
definition: Reaction of a nitroalkene with an alpha-isocyanoacetate to yield a substituted pyrrole.

label: pyrrole synthesis
definition: A reaction in which the intended product is a pyrrole that was not present in any of the reactants.

label: Béchamp reduction
definition: Reduction of an aromatic nitro compound to the corresponding arene by iron in some form in aqueous acid.

label: Beirut reaction
definition: Reaction of a benzofurazan 1-oxide with an enamine or enolate to yield a quinoxaline-1,4-dioxide.

label: Bénary reaction
definition: Reaction of a beta-aminovinylketone with a Grignard reagent to yield a substituted alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compound.

label: Feist-Bénary synthesis
definition: Reaction of an alpha-haloketone or alpha-haloether with a 1,3-dicarbonyl compound in the presence of pyridine to yield a substituted furan.

label: Boulton-Katritzky rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of a mancude eight-membered system containing a pentacycle into a different pentacycle where two of the ring atoms are ring atoms in the original molecule.

label: monocyclic Boulton-Katritzky rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of an azapentacycle containing a -N= system where there is an unsaturated three-heavy-atom substituent XYZ on the substituent double-bonded to the nitrogen, to a new azapentacycle where the pivotal nitrogen has broken its single bond and formed a new bond with atom Z.

label: bicyclic Boulton-Katritzky rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of a mancude bicyclo[4.3.0] compound with an unsaturated two-heavy-atom substituent on the six-membered ring alpha to the bridgehead to form a new mancude bicyclo[4.3.0] compound where the five-membered ring has been formed from the two-heavy-atom substituent and the original five-membered ring has been broken.

label: Mukaiyama-Hoshino method
definition: Reaction of a primary nitro compound with phenyl isocynatate and triethylamine to yield a nitrile oxide.

label: Nef synthesis
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde or ketone with a sodium acetylide to yield a propargyl alcohol.

label: Krapcho decarboxylation
definition: Reaction of an ester with an electron-withdrawing group alpha- to the ester functionality with a metal halide or pseudohalide to yield the shorter homologous system.

label: Cadogan cyclization
definition: Reaction of an aromatic nitro compound with an unsaturated substituent at the alpha-position to form a fused-ring nitrogen heterocycle.

label: Cadogan indole synthesis
definition: A Cadogan cyclization that results in an indole.

label: Benkeser reduction
definition: Addition of hydrogen atoms to unsaturated rings by means of alkali metals in low-molecular weight amines.

label: Madelung indole synthesis
definition: Reaction of an N-(2-alkylphenyl)alkanamide with strong base to form an indole.

label: Smith indole synthesis
definition: Reaction of a N-(2-alkylphenyl)-trimethylsilylamine with two equivalents of organolithium and an ester or carboxylic acid to form an indole.

label: Mumm rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of an acyl imidate to form a carboximide.

label: Chapman rearrangement
definition: Rearrangement of an aryl imino ether to yield an N,N-diaryl amide.

label: Bergmann azlactone peptide synthesis
definition: Reaction of an acetylated amino acid with an aldehyde then with a second amino acid, then hydrogenation and hydrolysis to form a dipeptide. It proceeds via an azlactone intermediate.

label: Bergmann degradation
definition: Removal of a terminal amino acid residue from a peptide by benzoylation, azidation, treatment with benzyl alcohol, hydrogenation and hydrolysis.

label: terminal amino-acid residue cleaving reaction
definition: Removal of a terminal amino acid residue from a peptide.

label: acridine synthesis
definition: Ring formation reaction where the intended product is an acridine.

label: Bernthsen acridine synthesis
definition: Reaction of a diarylamine in an organic acid or anhydride in the presence of zinc chloride to yield a 9-substituted acridine.

label: pyridine synthesis
definition: Ring formation reaction where the intended product is a pyridine.

label: carbazole synthesis
definition: Ring formation reaction where the intended product is a carbazole.

label: Betti reaction
definition: Reaction of a phenol, an aromatic aldehyde and a primary aromatic or heterocyclic amine to yield a Betti's base, also known as an alpha-aminobenzylphenol.

label: Bischler-Möhlau indole synthesis
definition: Reaction of a terminal hydroxyketone or terminal halogenoketone with an aromatic amine to yield a 2-substituted indole.

label: Camps quinoline synthesis
definition: Cyclisation of an o-acylaminoacetophenone in alcoholic sodium hydroxide to form a substituted hydroxyquinoline.

label: Castro-Stephens coupling
definition: Palladium-catalysed coupling of copper(I) acetylides with aryl halides to yield arylacetylenes.

label: Chichibabin pyridine synthesis
definition: Reaction of carbonyl compounds with ammonia or an amine to yield a pyridine.

label: Chichibabin reaction
definition: Reaction of an azacycle with an alkali-metal amide to yield an aminated azacycle.

label: Bodroux reaction
definition: Reaction of a carboxylic ester with a magnesium iodide alkylamide to yield a carboxamide.

label: Bogert-Cook synthesis
definition: Condensation of beta-phenylethylmagnesium bromide with a cyclohexanone followed by cyclisation and dehydration in concentrated sulfuric acid to yield an octahydrophenanthrene.

label: Bohn-Schmidt reaction
definition: Reaction of a hydroxylated anthraquinone with fuming sulfuric acid or sulfuric acid in boric acid in the presence of a mercury or selenium catalyst to form a more hydroxylated anthraquinone.

label: Boord olefin synthesis
definition: Etherification of an aldehyde followed by bromination and reaction with a Grignard reagent followed by treatment with zinc to eliminate the halogen and alkoxy groups to produce an olefin.

label: Borsche-Drechsel cyclisation
definition: Rearrangement of a cyclohexanone phenylhydrazone followed by oxidation to yield a carbazole.

label: Bouveault aldehyde synthesis
definition: Reaction of an organomagnesium or organolithium compound with an N,N'-disubstituted formamide to yield the next homologous aldehyde.

label: Bradsher cyclisation
definition: Diels-Alder reaction of a dienophile with a cationic aromatic azadiene to yield a bridged-ring system.

label: Bradsher reaction
definition: Reaction of an ortho-acyldiarylmethane, catalysed by acid, to yield an anthracene.

label: Parham cyclisation
definition: Reaction of a bromoarene that has an electrophilic group at the para-position with lithium bromide or lithium iodide followed by intramolecular cyclisation to yield a fused-ring system.

label: Quelet reaction
definition: Reaction of an aliphatic aldehyde with a phenolic ether and hydrochloric acid to yield an alpha-chloroalkylphenolic ether.

label: Chugaev reaction
definition: Elimination of a water molecule from a secondary alcohol to yield an olefin.

label: Cope elimination
definition: Elimination of a hydroxylamine from an amine oxide by pyrolysis to yield an olefin.

label: Sugasawa reaction
definition: Reaction of an aniline with a nitrile in the presence of boron trichloride and a Lewis acid to yield an ortho-acylated aniline.

label: palladium-catalysed Kumada coupling
definition: A cross-coupling reaction between a Grignard reagent and an activated (aryl or vinyl) halide or triflate. The reaction is catalysed by a palladium catalyst.

label: nickel-catalysed Kumada coupling
definition: A cross-coupling reaction between a Grignard reagent and an activated (aryl or vinyl) halide or triflate. The reaction is catalysed by a nickel catalyst.

label: Eastwood reaction
definition: Reaction of a 1,2-diol with triethoxymethane under heat to yield an olefin.

label: Saegusa oxidation
definition: Reaction of silyl enol ethers with palladium acetate in acetonitrile to yield an alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone.

label: Sandmeyer diphenylurea isatin synthesis
definition: Reaction of diphenylthiourea with lead carbonate and potassium cyanide to form a cyanoformidine followed by ring closure and acid hydrolysis to yield isatin.

label: Sandmeyer isonitrosoacetanilide isatin synthesis
definition: Reaction of aniline, hydroxylamine and chloral hydrate to form an aniline intermediate followed by cyclization in acid and hydrolysis to yield isatin.

label: Sarett oxidation
definition: Oxidation of an alcohol with a pyridine-chromium trioxide complex to yield a carbonyl compound.

label: Sarett primary alcohol oxidation
definition: Oxidation of a primary alcohol with a pyridine-chromium trioxide complex to yield an aldehyde.

label: Sarett secondary alcohol oxidation
definition: Oxidation of a secondary alcohol with a pyridine-chromium trioxide complex to yield a ketone.

label: Collins oxidation
definition: Oxidation of a primary alcohol with a pyridine-chromium trioxide complex in dichloromethane to yield an aldehyde.

label: reductive imination
definition: A functional group modification where a carbonyl oxygen is replaced by an imine.

label: Leuckart aldehyde reaction
definition: Reductive amination of an aldehyde with ammonium formate or formamide to form an amine.

label: Leuckart ketone reaction
definition: Reductive amination of a ketone with ammonium formate or formamide to form an amine.

label: Appel bromination with tetrabromomethane
definition: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl bromide with triphenylphosphine and tetrabromomethane.

label: Appel bromination with bromine
definition: Conversion of an alcohol to an alkyl bromide with triphenylphosphine and bromine.

label: Corey-Fuchs terminal dibromoolefination
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde with tetrabromomethane and triphenylphosphine to yield a terminal dibromoolefin.

label: obsolete: iodo Heck reaction/chloride Finkelstein reaction
obsolete: triflyloxy Heck reaction/bromide Finkelstein reaction
obsolete: bromo Heck-type reaction/sulfonate ester Finkelstein reaction
obsolete: bromo Suzuki coupling/iodo Heck reaction
obsolete: bromo Suzuki-type coupling/triflyloxy Heck reaction
obsolete: chloro Sonogashira coupling/chloro Heck-type reaction
obsolete: chloro Suzuki-type coupling/bromo Heck reaction
cyclic Beckmann rearrangement
definition: A Beckmann rearrangement which produces a cyclic product.

label: Dess-Martin aldehyde oxidation
definition: A Dess-Martin oxidation of a primary alcohol to an aldehyde.

label: Dess-Martin ketone oxidation
definition: A Dess-Martin oxidation of a secondary alcohol to a ketone.

label: Friedlander quinoline synthesis
definition: Synthesis of a quinoline by reaction of a 2-aminobenzaldehyde with a ketone.

label: Gewald reaction
definition: Reaction of an aldehyde or ketone with an alpha-cyanoester to form a 2-aminothiophene.

label: iodo Sonogashira coupling
definition: A Sonogashira coupling where the aryl halide is an aryl iodide.

label: iodo Suzuki coupling
definition: A Suzuki coupling where the halide is an iodide.

label: iodo Suzuki-type coupling
definition: A Suzuki-type coupling where the halide is an iodide.

label: iodolactonization
definition: A halolactionization where the halide is an iodide.

label: Niementowski quinazoline synthesis
definition: An azacycle synthesis where the product is a 4-oxo-3,4-dihydroquinazoline and the reactants are a 2-aminobenzoic acid and an amide.

label: Niementowski quinoline synthesis
definition: A quinoline synthesis where the product is a gamma-hydroxyquinoline and the reactants are a 2-aminobenzoic acid and an aldehyde or ketone.

label: Pomeranz–Fritsch reaction
definition: An azacycle synthesis where the product is an isoquinoline and the reactants are a benzaldehyde and a 2,2-dialkoxyethylamine.

label: Steglich esterification
definition: An O-acylation to ester where dicyclohexylcarbodiimide is used as a coupling reagent and 4-dimethylaminopyridine as a catalyst.

label: triflyloxy Sonogashira coupling
definition: A Sonogashira coupling where the role of the halide is played by a triflyloxy group.

label: triflyloxy Suzuki coupling
definition: A Suzuki coupling where the rule of the halide is played by a triflyloxy group.

label: triflyloxy Suzuki-type coupling
definition: A Suzuki-type coupling where the rule of the halide is played by a triflyloxy group.

label: Wenker synthesis
definition: Synthesis of an aziridine by dehydrating a beta aminoalcohol with sulfuric acid.

label: Hinsberg thiophene synthesis
definition: Reaction of an alpha-diketone with a dialkyl thiodiacetate to form a thiophene dicarboxylic acid.

label: Suzuki-type coupling
definition: The coupling of a boronic acid or boronic ester with an unsaturated halide, triflate or sulfonate.

label: halolactonization
definition: Addition of a carboxylic acid oxygen and halogen across a carbon-carbon double bond to form a lactone.

label: Ciamician-Dennstedt rearrangement
definition: Expansion of a pyrrole ring by heating with a halogeno compond to form a 3-halogenopyridine.

label: Claisen-Schmidt condensation
definition: Condensation of an aromatic aldehyde with an aliphatic aldehyde or ketone in the presence of a relatively strong base to from an alpha,beta-unsaturated aldehyde or ketone.

label: Corey-Bakshi-Shibata reduction
definition: Enantioselective reduction of a ketone to an alcohol with a chiral oxazaborolidine.

label: Johnson-Corey-Chaykovsky reaction
definition: Reaction of a sulfur ylide with a double-bond to produce a three-membered ring.

label: Johnson-Corey-Chaykovsky epoxide synthesis
definition: Reaction of a sulfur ylide with a ketone to produce an epoxide.

label: Johnson-Corey-Chaykovsky cyclopropane synthesis
definition: Reaction of a sulfur ylide with an olefin to produce a cyclopropane.

label: Johnson-Corey-Chaykovsky aziridine synthesis
definition: Reaction of a sulfur ylide with an imine to produce an aziridine.

label: Johnson-Corey-Chaykovsky thiirane synthesis
definition: Reaction of a sulfur ylide with a thione to produce a thiirane.

label: cross-coupling
definition: A joining reaction where a carbon-carbon bond is formed between unlike moieties.

label: Liebeskind-Srogl coupling
definition: A cross-coupling reaction where a thioester and a boronic acid react to form a ketone. It uses a metal catalyst.

label: Fukuyama coupling
definition: A cross-coupling reaction where a thioester and an organozinc halide react to form a ketone. It uses a palladium catalyst.

label: chloride Finkelstein reaction
definition: The conversion of an alkyl chloride to an alkyl iodide by SN2 substitution.The reaction relies upon the equilibrium being pushed to completion by precipitation.

label: bromide Finkelstein reaction
definition: The conversion of an alkyl bromide to an alkyl iodide by SN2 substitution.The reaction relies upon the equilibrium being pushed to completion by precipitation.

label: sulfonate ester Finkelstein reaction
definition: The conversion of an alkyl sulfonate ester to an alkyl iodide by SN2 substitution.The reaction relies upon the equilibrium being pushed to completion by precipitation.

label: Sandmeyer fluorination
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(I) fluoride to form an aromatic fluoride.

label: Sandmeyer chlorination
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(I) chloride to form an aromatic chloride.

label: Sandmeyer bromination
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(I) bromide to form an aromatic bromide.

label: Sandmeyer iodination
definition: An aromatic substitution reaction where an aryl diazonium salt reacts with a copper(I) iodide to form an aromatic iodide.

label: imidazole synthesis
definition: A planned reaction in which one of the products contains an imidazole system that was not in any of the reactants.

label: indazole synthesis
definition: A planned reaction in which one of the products contains an indazole system that was not in any of the reactants.

label: piperazine synthesis
definition: A planned reaction in which one of the products contains a piperazine system that was not in any of the reactants.

label: piperidine synthesis
definition: A planned reaction in which one of the products contains a piperidine system that was not in any of the reactants.

label: pyrrolidine synthesis
definition: A planned reaction in which one of the products contains a pyrrolidine system that was not in any of the reactants.

label: Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction
definition: An azacycle synthesis in which pyrrolidines or piperidines are formed by decomposition of protonated N-haloamines.

label: Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag piperidine synthesis
definition: A Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction in which the product is a piperidine.

label: Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag pyrrolidine synthesis
definition: A Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction in which the product is a pyrrolidine.

label: Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky reaction
definition: An alpha-halogenation reaction where a carboxylic acid reacts with dibromine to form an alpha-bromocarboxylic acid.

label: Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction of an aldehyde
definition: Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction of an aldehyde to the corresponding primary alcohol.

label: Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction of a ketone
definition: Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley reduction of a ketone to the corresponding secondary alcohol.

label: Heck-type reaction
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction where an organohalide or triflate couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin.

label: obsolete: bromo Heck reaction
definition: A Heck reaction where an organobromide couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin, catalysed by palladium.

label: chloro Heck reaction
definition: A Heck reaction where an organochloride couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin, catalysed by palladium.

label: iodo Heck reaction
definition: A Heck reaction where an organoiodide couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin, catalysed by palladium.

label: triflyloxy Heck reaction
definition: A Heck reaction where an organotriflate couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin, catalysed by palladium.

label: bromo Heck-type reaction
definition: A Heck-type reaction where an organobromide couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin.

label: chloro Heck-type reaction
definition: A Heck-type reaction where an organochloride couples to a terminal olefin to produce an internal olefin.

label: acyclic Beckmann rearrangement
definition: A Beckmann rearrangement where an acyclic amide is formed.

label: Baeyer–Emmerling indole synthesis
definition: An indole synthesis where an ortho-nitrocinnamic acid reacts with iron powder in a basic solution to form an indole.

label: bromo Sonogashira coupling
definition: A Sonogashira coupling where the aryl halide is an aryl bromide.

label: bromo Suzuki coupling
definition: A Suzuki coupling where the halide is a bromide.

label: bromo Suzuki-type coupling
definition: A Suzuki-type coupling where the halide is a bromide.

label: bromolactonization
definition: A halolactionization where the halide is a bromide.

label: chloro Sonogashira coupling
definition: A Sonogashira coupling where the aryl halide is an aryl chloride.

label: chloro Suzuki coupling
definition: A Suzuki coupling where the halide is a chloride.

label: chloro Suzuki-type coupling
definition: A Suzuki-type coupling where the halide is a chloride.

label: chlorolactonization
definition: A halolactionization where the halide is a chloride.

label: Conrad-Limpach cyclisation
definition: Reaction of an arylamine wtih a beta-ketoester to yield a 4-hydroxyquinoline.

label: Combes quinoline synthesis
definition: Reaction of a beta-diketone with a primary arylamine to yield a quinoline. It is acid-catalysed.

label: Prato reaction
definition: A 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition reaction where a fullerene reacts with an aldehyde and an amino acid to form a pyrrolidinofullerene.

label: Piancatelli rearrangement
definition: A rearrangement of a 2-furylcarbinol to a 4-hydroxycyclopentenone. It is acid-catalysed.

label: retro-Prato reaction
definition: A ring-breaking reaction where a pyrrolidinofullerene yields a fullerene. The substituted fullerene is heated with a dipolarophile and a catalyst.

label: Bingel reaction
definition: A cycloaddition where a fullerene reacts with diethyl bromomalonate to form a methanofullerene.

label: Wacker cyclisation
definition: A cyclisation where a hydroxyl group acts as the nucleophile in a Wacker-Tsuji oxidation accompanied by beta-hydride elimination, forming a ring.

label: aza-Wacker cyclisation
definition: A cyclisation where an amino group acts as the nucleophile in a Wacker-Tsuji oxidation accompanied by beta-hydride elimination, forming a ring.

label: Lemieux-Johnson oxidation
definition: A cleaving reaction where an olefin is dihydroxylated with osmium tetroxide and undergoes a Malaprade reaction to yield two aldehydes, two ketones, or an aldehyde and a ketone.

label: Malaprade reaction
definition: A cleaving reaction of a 1,2-diol using periodic acid to give the corresponding carbonyl compounds.

label: Fenton 1,2-glycol oxidation
definition: The oxidation of a 1,2-glycol with Fenton's reagent (hydrogen peroxide and iron salts) to give an alpha-hydroxy aldehyde.

label: Fenton alpha-hydroxy carboxylic acid oxidation
definition: The oxidation of an alpha-hydroxy carboxylic acid with Fenton's reagent to give an alpha-keto carboxylic acid.

label: Fenton hydrogen peroxide reduction
definition: The reaction of the components of Fenton's reagent, viz. reduction of hydrogen peroxide by Fe(II) , producing an hydroxyl radical.

label: Mukaiyama hydration
definition: The Mukaiyama hydration is an organic reaction involving formal addition of an equivalent of water across an alkene by the action of catalytic bis(acetylacetonato)cobalt(II) complex, phenylsilane and atmospheric oxygen.

label: Favorskii reaction
definition: A reaction between an alkyne and a carbonyl group under basic conditions in which a terminal alkyne serves as the CH-acidic moiety. When the carbonyl is an aldehyde, the 'Favorskii rearrangement' (RXNO:0000385) takes place and leads to an enone.

label: Diimide reduction
definition: Reductions with diimide are a chemical reactions that convert unsaturated organic compounds to reduced alkane products. In the process, diimide is oxidized to dinitrogen.

label: carbonyl group protection
definition: A protection reaction where the reactive centre is a carbonyl oxygen.

label: acetal protection
definition: The acid-catalyzed formation of an acetal (or ketal) to protect a carbonyl group by nucleophilic addition of an alcohol to an aldehyde (or ketone) with the elimination of water.

label: Miyaura borylation
definition: A chemical reaction that allows the generation of boronates from vinyl or aryl halides with the cross-coupling of bis(pinacolato)diboron under basic conditions.

label: Corey-Winter elimination
definition: A chemical reaction for the stereoselective synthesis of an olefin from a 1,2-diol.

label: Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation
definition: The Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation reaction is a method for the decarbonylation of aldehydes and some acyl chlorides.

label: Corey-Seebach reaction
definition: A chemical reaction in which ketones are synthesized from formaldehyde or other aldehydes, involving a temporary repolarization of a carbonyl group.

label: nitroxide exchange reaction
definition: Nitroxide exchange reactions are based on thermal C–O bond homolysis of alkoxyamines, which leads to transient carbon-centred radicals and persistent nitroxide radicals. Usually these carbon centred radicals are quickly trapped by the nitroxide radicals and reform the alkoxyamines.

label: Baeyer pyridine synthesis
definition: The conversion of pyran or pyrone (pyranone) derivatives to the corresponding pyridine derivative.

label: Corey-Kim oxidation
definition: Oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols via their alkoxysulfonium salts. Upon the addition of base, the salt rearranges intramolecularly to aldehydes and ketones, respectively.

label: Corey-Suggs oxidation
definition: Oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones, respectively, through the use of pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC).

label: Corey-Schmidt oxidation
definition: Oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones, respectively, through the use of pyridinium dichromate (PDC).

label: Albright-Goldman oxidation
definition: A particularly mild oxidation method in which, for example, dimethyl sulfoxide and acetic anhydride can be used as oxidizing agents to selectively produce aldehydes from primary alcohols without further oxidation to carboxylic acids. Analogously, secondary alcohols can be oxidized to form ketones.

label: Rubottom oxidation
definition: The Rubottom oxidation is a useful, high-yielding chemical reaction between silyl enol ethers and peroxy acids to give the corresponding α-hydroxy carbonyl product.

label: Groebke-Blackburn-Bienaymé reaction
definition: A three-component reaction (3CR) of a heterocyclic amidine, an aldehyde, and an isocyanide, resulting in a cyclic compound with imidazo[1,2-a] heterocycle as the core structure.

label: Baeyer oxindole synthesis
definition: The preparation of oxindole/indolin-2-one from 2-nitrophenylacetic acid by reduction of the nitro group and subsequent dehydration.

label: Leimgruber-Batcho indole synthesis
definition: Condensation of a formamide acetal with a derivative of o-nitrotoluene to yield an enamine intermediate that subsequently undergoes reductive cyclization to give the corresponding indole.

label: IBX oxidation
definition: Oxidation of alcohols to the corresponding carbonyl product using 2-iodoxybenzoic acid (IBX) as oxidizing agent.

label: decarboxylative cross-coupling
definition: A chemical reaction in which a carboxylic acid (or its salt) reacts with an organic (alkyl or aryl) halide to form a new carbon-carbon bond with concomitant loss of CO2. In addition, the reaction requires a metal catalyst, a base and an oxidizing agent.

label: Balsohn alkylation
definition: A carbon-carbon coupling reaction between an arene and an alkene to give a substituted arene. The reaction is catalysed by a Lewis acid.

label: Kabachnik-Fields reaction
definition: A three-component condensation reaction in which primary or secondary amines react with a carbonyl compound (aldehyde or ketone) and a dialkyl phosphonate (also called dialkyl phosphite) to form an alpha-aminophosphonate.

label: Baekeland-Manasse-Lederer reaction
definition: A polymerisation reaction in which phenol and formaldehyde react together in a chain reaction to form a phenol formaldehyde resin (also infrequently called phenoplast).

label: Aston-Greenburg rearrangement
definition: The rearrangement of an alpha-haloketone into an ester with a tertiary alpha-carbon atom by intramolecular migration of an alkyl or aryl group.

label: Baddeley isomerization
definition: The rearrangement/migration of alkyl groups in polyalkylbenzenes or polynuclear aromatic compounds in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride or the mixture of protonic acid and Lewis acid.

label: Fukuyama reduction
definition: The reduction of a thioester to an aldehyde by a silyl hydride in the presence of catalytic amounts of palladium.

label: Dötz reaction
definition: A three component cyclization of an aromatic or vinylic alkoxy pentacarbonyl chromium carbene complex, an alkyne and carbon monoxide to give a Cr(CO)3 coordinated phenol.

label: Kulinkovich reaction
definition: The formation of a substituted cyclopropanol by reaction of a carboxylic ester with a Grignard reagent (carrying a hydrogen atom in the beta-position) in the presence of a titanium(IV) alkoxide as catalyst. A modified cyclopropanol product can be generated by addition of a terminal alkene as a further reactant.

label: Kulinkovich-de Meijere reaction
definition: The formation of a substituted cyclopropylamine by reaction of an amide with a Grignard reagent (carrying a hydrogen atom in the beta-position) in the presence of a titanium(IV) alkoxide as catalyst.

label: Kulinkovich-Szymoniak reaction
definition: The formation of a substituted primary cyclopropylamine by reaction of a nitrile with a Grignard reagent (carrying a hydrogen atom in the beta-position) in the presence of a titanium(IV) alkoxide as catalyst.

label: Jacobsen epoxidation
definition: Chiral (salen)manganese(III)-catalyzed asymmetric epoxidation of alkenes. In addition to the catalyst, stoichiometric amounts of an oxidant (e.g. sodium hypochlorite) are required.

label: Shi epoxidation
definition: Organocatalytic enantioselective epoxidation of trans- and tri-substituted olefins using a fructose derived chiral ketone catalyst and potassium peroxomonosulfate or H2O2 as an oxidant.

label: Asinger reaction
definition: A multicomponent reaction (A-4CR) in which 3-thiazolines (2,5-dihydro-1,3-thiazoles) or mixtures of several 3-thiazolines are synthesized from elemental sulfur, an alpha-substitutable ketone or aldehyde, another carbonyl component and ammonia.

label: haloform reaction
definition: Cleavage of methyl ketones (i.e. oxidative demethylation) with halogens (or hypohalites) in the presence of a base to give a carboxylic acid and haloform (trihalomethane).

label: Atherton-Todd reaction
definition: The conversion of dialkyl phosphites (dialkyl phosphonates) to dialkyl chlorophosphates using tetrachloromethane and a base (usually a primary, secondary or tertiary amine).

label: Baeyer diarylmethane synthesis
definition: Synthesis of diarylmethane derivatives using an aldehyde and an aromatic compound at low temperature in an acidic medium.

label: silyl deprotection
definition: A hydroxyl group deprotection in which an silyl ether group is transformed into a hydroxy group.
